title: Cosmos Air Hockey — drag your mallet to slam a glowing puck past the CPU
summary: A real, self-contained playable air hockey match in one HTML file with zero dependencies. Drag your bottom mallet to defend your goal and slam a glowing puck past a defending-and-attacking CPU on the top; the puck carries mallet momentum on contact, banks off the side rails, and the live scoreboard tracks first-to-7. Keyboard plus drag/touch control, win/lose banner, and instant restart. Ships with a deterministic self-playing attract demo where both mallets are AI rallying and scoring on the seeded puck.
usage_notes: RUN: open index.html (double-click) — no server, no deps. CONTROLS: drag / touch anywhere on the lower half to move your mallet, or arrows / WASD nudge it; R restarts. GOAL: knock the puck into the CPU's goal at the top of the table; first side to 7 points wins; the CPU scores on you through the bottom goal. KEY TECHNIQUES: fixed-timestep deterministic physics — circle-vs-circle mallet collision imparts mallet velocity to the puck, rail reflections, and goal-gap detection; each mallet runs a defend-or-attack AI driven only by a mulberry32 seed with per-serve aim biases so goals happen reproducibly. In AUTO both mallets use that same AI so the rally is fully reproducible, and frame 0 is offset mid-rally. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 self-plays + window.__seek(t). TUNE: WIN target, MR/PR sizes, GW goal width, puck speed cap in collideMallet(), mallet maxSp, T_OFFSET (frame-0 offset).
